LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT STEVEN LAU'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE ENTRY OF DEFAULT IS GRANTED. IN LIGHT OF THE LIBERAL POLICY OF HEARING CASES ON THE MERITS, THE COURT FINDS THAT PLAINTIFF DEMONSTRATES EXCUSABLE NEGLECT, BROUGHT THIS MOTION WITHIN A REASONABLE TIME AND PROVIDED A PROPOSED PLEADING. DEFENDANT LAU SHALL REMIT $500 TO PLAINTIFF FOR THE COSTS OF OBTAINING THE DEFAULT. SEE ROGALSKI V. NABERS CADILLAC (1992) 11 CAL.APP.4TH 816, 822-823. DEFENDANT MUST FILE HIS PLEADING ON OR BEFORE SEPTEMBER 21, 2015. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH; CLERK: SEAN KANE; REPORTER: MELANIE GHENO, CSR #7489 (302/EHG)

MOTION TO CONTINUE JURY TRIAL
Off Calendar Feb-23-2016 Text Ruling

Notice Of Motion For Leave Of Court To File Cross-Complaint
Off Calendar Dec-04-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. JOHN T. HENDRICK OF HENDRICKS MURRY, TEL. NO. 1-415-986-9800 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT;
NO OTHER APPEARANCES ON THE MATTER.

RULING - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT STEVEN LAU'S Motion For Leave Of Court To File Cross-Complaint is granted. Judicial economy and the strong policies favoring granting amendments to pleadings and deciding cases on their merits support granting the motion. Plaintiff has not shown that he will be prejudiced if the motion is granted. Plaintiff's request to severing the cross-complaint is denied. Severing the cross-complaint would disserve judicial economy. Plaintiff has waived any venue objection to the cross-complaint by filing his complaint in this county. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter : Melanie Gheno, CSR # 7489 =302/hk

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Oct-14-2015 Set For Trial On Feb-29-16 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

OBJECTION HEARING CALENDAR
Off Calendar Oct-28-2015 Off Calendar

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Default Judgment; Memorandum Of Points &Amp; Authorities
Off Calendar Sep-16-2015 Off Calendar

Notice Of Motion To Set Aside Entry Of Default
Off Calendar Sep-15-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Atty. RICHARD A. LAPPING, ESQ. of Law Office of Richard A. Lapping, Tel No. 415-399-1015 appearing for the Plaintiff;
Atty. JOHN T. HENDRICKS, ESQ. of HendricksMurry, P.C., Tel No. 415-986-9800 appearing for Defendant Steven Lau.

RULING - A R G U E D - The Court modified its tentative ruling, and ordered as follows:
DEFENDANT STEVEN LAU'S Motion To Set Aside Entry Of Default is granted. In light of the liberal policy of hearing cases on the merits, the court finds that plaintiff demonstrates excusable neglect, brought this motion within a reasonable time and provided a proposed pleading. Defendant Lau shall remit $500 to plaintiff for the costs of obtaining the default. See Rogalski v. Nabers Cadillac (1992) 11 Cal.App.4th 816, 822-823. Defendant must file his pleading on or before September 21, 2015. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Melanie Gheno, CSR #7489 (302/EHG)
